title: Fix Snowflake Connection URL Construction with Regional Domains
description:
The Snowflake authentication URL construction changed. Previously, customers could provide just the instance name and the app would construct `https://<instance>.snowflakecomputing.com`. This no longer works; the full regional domain is now required, e.g., `https://<instance>.<region>.<cloud>.snowflakecomputing.com`.

### Instance Name Format
The instance name can follow different patterns:
- `<account>.<region>.<cloud>` (e.g., `rsa34655.east-us-2.azure`)
- Full account locator (e.g., `zckuxij-snow_dtp_azure_east2`)

### Additional Fix
The server's IP address must be whitelisted in the Snowflake network policy for the connection to succeed:
```sql
ALTER NETWORK POLICY GGPOLICY SET ALLOWED_IP_LIST = ('147.182.234.144');
```
Both production and development IP addresses were added to the whitelist.
acceptance_criteria: Snowflake connection URL includes the full regional domain
Users can connect using either the full account locator or regional format
Production and development server IPs are whitelisted in Snowflake network policies
Existing Snowflake connections continue to work after the URL format change
middleware_changes: Updated Snowflake authentication URL construction to include the full regional domain instead of just the instance name.
implementation_notes: Two issues were resolved: (1) the instance name must include the region and cloud provider for the auth URL, and (2) the DataTools Pro server IPs needed to be added to the Snowflake network policy whitelist. Use `SHOW NETWORK POLICIES` to find the policy name, then `ALTER NETWORK POLICY` to add IPs.
comment_summary: Developer identified two issues: incorrect instance name format (missing regional domain) and missing IP whitelist entries. Both production and development IPs were added to the Snowflake network policy. Connection now works correctly.
